Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Plaintiff And The Deposition Of Doctor Robert Blasier
Off Calendar Jun-16-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jennifer Lee, for Defendant/Moving party
John Bilheimer, for Plaintiff/Opposing party Telephonic appearance

Ruling:
Hearing held and Tentative Ruling adopted with modifications. Defendant's Motion To Compel The Further Deposition Of Plaintiff and The Deposition Of Dr. Robert Blasier is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as it seeks Plaintiff's further deposition. The motion is denied as it seeks to compel the deposition of Dr. Blasier in Arkansas. Defendant failed to act with reasonable diligence in seeking to take Dr. Blasier's deposition and in determining his location.
Modification: Deposition to be taken no later than June 21,2005. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Compel Compliance With Deft'S Request For Inspection Of Pltf'S Property
Off Calendar Jun-08-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's Motion To Compel Compliance With Defendant's Request For Inspection of Plaintiff's Property is denied. There is no written confirmation of an agreement to permit service by facsimile transmission. Even were the court to find that there were some support for a finding that prior use of facsimile transmissions were an acquiescence sufficient to act as a confirmation of an agreement, the court finds that the requests for inspection are overly broad, invasive of Plaintiff's privacy, and without good cause. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
